_meta.description: Hand-curated deep content for the most-used modules. Each entry is structured exactly like the source PDFs in Context_System/Module_PDFs/ (verbatim from M08, same schema for the others). The engine uses this to expand response banks; modules without an entry here still work but only via the workbook-derived title and NCLEX domain.

M02.summary: Therapeutic communication: open-ended questions, reflection, silence, validation. SBAR handoff. Cultural humility. Trauma-informed care. De-escalation.
conditions: transfer to higher level of care
procedures: SBAR (Situation/Background/Assessment/Recommendation)
ISBARR variant
teach-back method
open-ended → focused → confirmation funnel
redFlags: yes/no answers from a patient who normally elaborates

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Conduct routine communication, document, escalate concerns to RN. Cannot independently lead complex goals-of-care discussions.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Lead SBAR handoff, conduct patient/family teaching, initiate goals-of-care conversations within scope, escalate to provider.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Lead family meetings, deliver bad news, coordinate goals-of-care with palliative/social work.

roleHooks.Patient: I appreciate you taking the time to listen. Most nurses just rush in.
roleHooks.Family: I just want someone to actually tell me what is happening.
roleHooks.Clinician: Give me an SBAR — situation, background, assessment, recommendation. Don't bury the lead.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: I'll need a quick handoff before I take them to imaging — anything I should know?

M03.summary: Standard precautions, transmission-based isolation (contact, droplet, airborne), PPE donning/doffing order, hand hygiene, sharps safety, environmental cleaning.
medications: soap (C. diff requires soap+water, not ABHR)
bleach wipes for C. diff rooms
procedures: PPE doffing sequence: gloves → goggles → gown → mask (last)
hand hygiene before donning + after doffing
needleless systems
redFlags: needlestick injury → immediate report + PEP if indicated

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Apply standard + transmission-based precautions, use PPE correctly, recognize and report breaches.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Initiate isolation orders within protocol, educate patient/family/visitors, coordinate with infection prevention.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Adjust isolation status, manage outbreak response, lead institutional infection-prevention initiatives.

roleHooks.Patient: Why is everyone wearing all that gear when they come in?
roleHooks.Family: Can I still touch him? Is it dangerous for me?
roleHooks.Clinician: Confirm the isolation type before you go in — droplet vs. contact changes the PPE.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: I need to know the isolation status before I bring the meal tray in.

M06.summary: Pharmacology principles: pharmacokinetics, pharmacodynamics, dose calculation, weight-based dosing, renal/hepatic adjustment, high-alert medications, look-alike-sound-alike pairs.
medications: high-alert: insulin, heparin, opioids, chemotherapy, concentrated electrolytes (KCl)
procedures: five rights (right patient, drug, dose, route, time) + extended (documentation, indication, response)
independent double-check for high-alert meds
med math: D/H × Q, weight-based mg/kg, drip rate calculation
redFlags: look-alike pair (e.g., hydromorphone vs. morphine) confused

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Calculate and administer routine ordered medications by approved routes; cannot administer IV push opioids in most states.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Administer all routes, IV push, titrate per protocol, manage smart pumps, perform independent double-checks.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Prescribe, adjust based on labs, manage anticoagulation/insulin titration protocols.

roleHooks.Patient: Wait — that's a different color than my usual pill. Is that the same thing?
roleHooks.Family: He's on so many medications now. What are these for?
roleHooks.Clinician: Run the math on me before you push anything high-alert.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy here — that combination has a known QT-prolongation interaction. I'd choose an alternative.

M07.summary: Routes of administration (PO, SL, buccal, IM, SC, IV, topical, transdermal, inhaled, rectal). Six rights. NG/G-tube administration. Crushing/splitting rules. Z-track. Insulin and heparin SC technique.
devices: Z-track needle technique for IM iron
procedures: barcode scan medication + patient
five rights at the bedside, not at the cart
verify allergies before each dose
Z-track for IM iron and other staining/irritant meds

scopeOfPractice.LPN: PO, SC, IM, topical, ophthalmic, otic, NG, rectal. Many states: cannot give IV push or initial IV piggyback.
scopeOfPractice.RN: All routes including IV push and titrated drips per protocol.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Order, adjust, prescribe controlled substances per DEA registration.

roleHooks.Patient: I can't swallow this with the tube — does it have to be the whole pill?
roleHooks.Family: Is there a liquid version of his blood pressure med?
roleHooks.Clinician: Hold that — confirm the route before you give it. The order said SL, not PO.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy can compound a suspension if the patient can't swallow tablets — let me know.

M08.summary: Acute vs. chronic pain, multimodal analgesia, opioid stewardship, PCA, epidural, non-pharmacologic methods, pain in special populations.
medications: acetaminophen (max 3-4 g/day, lower in liver disease)
opioids: morphine, hydromorphone, fentanyl, oxycodone, hydrocodone
adjuvants: gabapentin, pregabalin, duloxetine, amitriptyline, capsaicin
procedures: 0-10 numeric pain scale, FLACC (peds), PAINAD (dementia), Wong-Baker faces
multimodal stacking: acetaminophen + NSAID + opioid
PCA loading dose, basal rate, demand bolus
naloxone administration: 0.04-0.4 mg IV/IM/SC, repeat q2-3 min, watch for re-narcotization
redFlags: pinpoint pupils with sedation = opioid toxicity
opioid withdrawal: yawning, lacrimation, mydriasis, piloerection, GI symptoms

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Reassess pain after intervention, administer ordered analgesics by approved route. Cannot manage PCA programming or epidural in most states. Recognize and report respiratory depression.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Administer IV opioids, manage PCA, monitor epidural, titrate per protocol, administer naloxone, lead pain reassessment, request order changes.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Order regional anesthesia, manage acute pain service, prescribe controlled substances per DEA registration, initiate buprenorphine.

roleHooks.Patient: My pain is 10/10 and you're not listening.
roleHooks.Family: Why isn't anyone giving him anything? He's clearly hurting.
roleHooks.Clinician: Has she had her q4 dose? Let's add a scheduled NSAID and reassess.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Pharmacy — for renal/hepatic dose adjustment; PT for movement strategies; spiritual care for total pain.

M22.summary: Type 1, Type 2, gestational, DKA, HHS, hypoglycemia, sick-day rules, insulin types and onset/peak/duration, insulin pump, CGM.
conditions: DKA (T1, glucose>250, ketones+, anion gap, pH<7.35)
HHS (T2, glucose>600, no ketones, profound dehydration)
procedures: fingerstick glucose AC and HS
insulin dose calculation: correction + carb count
rule of 15 for hypoglycemia (15g carbs, recheck in 15 min)
DKA management: fluids first, then insulin drip, monitor K+
sick-day rules: never skip insulin
redFlags: hypoglycemia with altered mental status → IV dextrose or IM glucagon
anuria + altered mental status + glucose > 600 = HHS
sliding scale alone with no basal in T1DM = inadequate

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Fingerstick glucose, administer ordered insulin by SC route, recognize and treat hypoglycemia per protocol, education on routine self-management.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Manage IV insulin drips, titrate per protocol, manage DKA/HHS, comprehensive diabetes education, sick-day planning, foot/eye/kidney screening coordination.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Adjust insulin regimens, prescribe oral agents/GLP-1/SGLT2, manage transition between insulin and oral agents, manage gestational diabetes.

roleHooks.Patient: I haven't been able to eat since yesterday. Should I still take my insulin?
roleHooks.Family: She's sweating and confused — is her sugar low?
roleHooks.Clinician: What's the current glucose, ketones, and anion gap? We'll start fluids before insulin.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Diabetes educator here — let's talk about carb counting and the dose adjustment plan before discharge.

M32.summary: Sepsis (suspected infection + organ dysfunction), septic shock (vasopressor-requiring hypotension + lactate>2), Hour-1 bundle, qSOFA, fluid resuscitation, source control.
medications: broad-spectrum antibiotics within 1 hour (vancomycin + cefepime/piperacillin-tazobactam typical)
procedures: Hour-1 bundle: lactate, blood cultures BEFORE antibiotics, antibiotics, 30 mL/kg fluids if hypotensive or lactate>4, pressors for MAP<65 after fluids
qSOFA: RR≥22, AMS, SBP≤100
source control (drain abscess, remove infected line)
redFlags: peripheral hypoperfusion + warm extremities (early) → cold + mottled (late)

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Recognize early sepsis signs (fever/hypothermia, tachycardia, AMS), report immediately, support fluid administration and monitoring.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Initiate sepsis bundle, draw cultures, hang antibiotics, manage fluids and pressors per protocol, monitor lactate trend, escalate to ICU.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Diagnose, order workup, manage vasopressors, lead ICU resuscitation, source control, antibiotic stewardship.

roleHooks.Patient: I just feel really off, like I'm not myself. I'm cold but the nurse says I have a fever.
roleHooks.Family: He was fine yesterday. Now he doesn't even know who I am.
roleHooks.Clinician: Get me a lactate and blood cultures off two sites before we hang the antibiotics. Then 30 per kg of LR wide open.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Lab here — preliminary cultures at 24 hours show gram-positive cocci in clusters. I've called the team.

M39.summary: Mood disorders (MDD, bipolar, dysthymia), anxiety disorders, suicidal ideation assessment, therapeutic milieu, safety planning.
devices: safety-rounded environment (no cords, no breakable items)
PHQ-9, Columbia Suicide Severity Rating Scale
procedures: safety/contract for safety
PHQ-9 routinely on admission and as ordered
Columbia scale for SI severity
lithium level, watch for toxicity (>1.5 = tremor, GI, ataxia)
MAOI dietary restrictions (no aged cheese, wine)
redFlags: command auditory hallucinations to harm self/others
serotonin syndrome with multiple serotonergic agents

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Therapeutic communication, observe and document mood/affect/SI, administer ordered psychotropics, recognize and report safety concerns immediately.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Comprehensive psych assessment including SI/HI, lead milieu, manage psychotropic titration, develop safety plan with patient, coordinate with psychiatry.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: PMHNP/psychiatrist — diagnose, prescribe, manage involuntary holds, ECT planning, complex pharmacotherapy.

roleHooks.Patient: I just feel empty. Most days I don't see the point of getting out of bed.
roleHooks.Family: I'm scared he's going to hurt himself. He won't talk to me anymore.
roleHooks.Clinician: Have you done a Columbia? I want to know severity before we move forward.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Social work here — let's start the safety plan and identify a follow-up therapist before discharge.

M42.summary: End-of-life care, palliative care vs. hospice, advance directives, POLST/MOLST, code status, comfort care orders, family support, common end-of-life symptom management.
medications: d/c non-comfort meds (statins, BP, glucose monitoring)
devices: comfort cart / comfort care order set
procedures: clarify code status (full, DNR, DNI, DNAR, comfort care)
honor advance directive / POLST
family meeting with palliative team
post-death care: bathing, family time, paperwork, autopsy discussion
redFlags: family conflict over goals of care
patient wishes inconsistent with surrogate's directives
missed opportunity for symptom relief in final hours

scopeOfPractice.LPN: Provide comfort care, administer ordered comfort meds, support family presence, document symptom relief.
scopeOfPractice.RN: Lead comfort care, titrate symptom-relief medications per protocol, facilitate family meeting, perform post-death care, support family bereavement.
scopeOfPractice.Advanced: Lead goals-of-care conversations, write comfort care orders, coordinate with palliative/hospice teams, manage complex symptom syndromes.

roleHooks.Patient: I just don't want to be in pain anymore. Please.
roleHooks.Family: Is this the end? How long does she have?
roleHooks.Clinician: Let's transition to comfort. D/c monitoring, scheduled morphine, and call the chaplain.
roleHooks.AlliedHealth: Chaplain here — would you like me to come sit with the family for a while?
